entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 07:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL mid-season report: Hawks take flight, Kangaroos flop, and the Lions roar
  • VCAT bans addicted nurse who stole dying patients’ medication
  • Australia news LIVE: Energy market suspends trading to shore up electricity grid; Fair Work Commission raises minimum wage by 5.2 per cent; RBA predicts 7 per cent inflation by end of 2022
  • ‘Bias’: Developers eyeing untouched land sue Andrews government over Surf Coast border
  • UCI World Championships course preview
  • NRL eyes raids on rival codes, more expansion for NRLW
  • What tenants can do if their landlord hikes the rent
  • Gawn to miss key stretch; new Blue given a huge task; Bucks’ advice to young Roo; Freo lose a key piece
  • Masks no longer mandatory at airports from Friday
  • Minimum wage increases by 5.2 per cent
  • Cement truck crashes into Sydney home
  • Prime Minister Anthony Albanese scrambling to solve growing energy crisis
  • Instagram giving parents control
  • Derryn Hinch confirms his plans to run for Victorian parliament
  • Supermarkets freeze prices on home brand staples as affordability bites
  • Buzz Lightyear reaches for the stars but lands in a black hole
  • Australian researchers find parallels between Alzheimer’s and long-COVID ‘brain fog’
  • Optus diffuses claims ‘unfriendly nations’ entered satellite orbit
  • Europe, India, UK deals in sight by end of year, new trade minister hopes
  • Newlywed Britney Spears buys Thousand Oaks mansion for millions
  • Energy market suspended after chaos made it ‘impossible to operate’
  • Blackouts dodged as regulator demands answers from power companies
  • Lowest-paid workers to receive 5.2% wage rise: Fair Work Commission
  • NSW will need Narrabri gas, federal resources minister says
  • Victoria may need to ease gas ban to avoid energy supply issues
  • Coalition’s ‘warmongering rhetoric’ backfired on Morrison: Labor campaign chief

  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox

  • Shorten promises new approach to ‘sustainability’ of the NDIS
  • Best of cartoons, June 14, 2022
  • ‘We’ll do what’s necessary’: RBA’s Lowe flags 7 per cent inflation, more rate hikes
  • Federal politicians awarded 2.75 per cent pay rise
  • Secret report reveals controversial NSW rail corporation told to ‘reframe’ its battered image
  • Ex-councillor angry $170k developer ‘bribes’ might be revealed: ICAC
  • Judge rules against new witness evidence at Chris Dawson murder trial
  • Why do private girls’ schools outrank boys when it comes to the HSC?
  • Secret plans to sell, rezone and develop land across Sydney railways
  • From the Archives, 1992: Artist’s death a shock, but no surprise
  • In the Herald: June 15, 1928
  • Ambulance waits the longest on record as life-threatening calls hit 10-year high
  • Why get a gong when you can ring?
  • Land tax not a magic bullet for housing affordability
  • ‘Deeply sorry’: Brazil apologises for telling journalist’s family bodies were found in Amazon
  • ‘No such convict here’: Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Biden breaks Saudi ‘pariah’ pledge to talk oil with crown prince
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Deportation flight from England to Rwanda cancelled
  • Duke and Duchess of Cambridge mark the fifth anniversary of the Grenfell Tower fire
  • ‘Friendliest of wars’ ends: Canada and Denmark sign deal over island
  • Family waits for ID of bodies found in Amazon search for missing pair
  • WHO to decide if monkeypox is an emergency
  • In Japan, online insults punishable by up to a year in prison
  • Britain prevented from sending first plane of refugees to Rwanda
  • Not actual legal advice: AI model tries to re-create mind of Ruth Bader Ginsburg
  • Happy, the elephant, isn’t a person, a top New York court rules
  • Trump skewered by closest advisors during Capitol riot hearing
  • Ukraine must maintain Western attention to win this war
  • The best photos from around the world
  • Pope criticises Russia over cruelty but says war ‘perhaps provoked’
  • Pharmacist admits burning down partner’s Brisbane home
  • New Brisbane cycling venue on track to foster Olympic stars
  • Tip of the iceberg: Let us explain why more vegie prices will rise
  • Brisbane raises rates by almost 5 per cent in record $4 billion budget
  • Where did all the power go? What caused the east coast energy crisis
  • Man arrested over involvement in three kidnappings across Sydney
  • Perth Wildcats sign three-time NBL champion
  • Stamp tax debate: Victoria faces a great property tax opportunity
  • Confidence crash: Terrified consumers shut their wallets and fear for the future
  • Yes, minimum wage rises can hurt jobs, but not this one
  • ‘Tip over the edge’: 5.2% wage rise hard to absorb, employers argue
  • Aussie bitcoin mogul in talks with US prosecutors after co-accused plead guilty
  • The threat of Europe breaking apart is rising as old ghosts emerge
  • ASX extends losses as Wall Street keeps falling and bitcoin swings
  • Former CBA subsidiary fined $1.71m after charging dead people fees
  • What’s stagflation, and what would it mean for you?
  • How CPA knowledge can impact tomorrow
  • The future of accounting in Australia
  • The crypto crash is a wake-up call for the deluded
  • Major retailers using facial recognition technology on unsuspecting customers: Choice
  • What we weren’t told before the election: taxes to rise, not fall
  • Development sites valued at $510m to test market appetite
  • ‘Bear-market blues’ test mettle of devout bitcoin believers
  • ‘Hits just keep coming’: Inflation fears could spark retail meltdown
  • ‘Walking a tightrope’: Recession fears wipe billions off the market
  • Updating Elvis: how Baz Luhrmann brought the King’s music into 2022
  • German drama Ku’Damm is more than just a feminist answer to Mad Men
  • Who’s the suspect in The Twelve; an artist accused of murder or the jury?
  • New Borgen pulls no punches depicting women, power and climate policy
  • The Crown’s breakout star Erin Doherty swaps young royal for imposter
  • Can a 17-year-old beat veteran ninja warriors to the very top?
  • ‘Victory for spinster theatregoers’ after Arts Centre denied tourist a ticket
  • A Squid Game reality show? Did Netflix miss the point of its biggest ever release?
  • Men is a horror movie about misogyny, but does the story lead us astray?
  • Twenty-five years after The Castle the Australian dream is well and truly dead
  • Gangsters go wild as Peaky Blinders gives fans a bloody send-off
  • Sony’s first female boss takes control almost a year after Handlin’s departure
  • Gemma Owen's ex-boyfriend enters the Villa
  • Chill out, what ya yellin’ for? Avril was the best, and we took her for granted
  • ‘Nineteen years of silence is our finest work’: Beloved satirists TISM reform after two decades
  • Computer mouses or computer mice? Let the people decide
  • ‘None of us knew’: How Wil Anderson surprised David Wenham on his ABC special
  • First look at Below Deck Season 7
  • ‘We promise we will return someday’: K-pop group BTS take a break to pursue solo projects
  • Instagram launches parental controls after mental health criticism
  • National weather forecast for Wednesday June 15
  • Geographe Bay a family-friendly destination for more than just humans
  • Hot, young, dead too soon – why these wrens’ climate future should worry us all
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine takes painful losses in east to wear down Russia
  • ‘Zero covid’ has many in China dreaming of leaving
  • The case for and against Biden visiting Saudi Arabia
  • Russia made nearly $100B from fuel exports in war’s first 100 days, report says
  • Casualties rise as Russia makes incremental gains in east, Pentagon official says

  • Navalny reportedly moved to high-security prison infamous for abuse

  • N.C. school leaders accused of ignoring abuse have immunity, court rules
  • Most Americans oppose trans athletes in female sports, poll finds
  • ‘How to Murder Your Husband’ writer sentenced to life for killing husband
  • Fans told Lizzo a word in her song was offensive. She changed the lyrics.
  • Idaho police get death threats after Patriot Front arrests, chief says

  • Primary elections live updates: Tom Rice ousted in S.C. after Trump impeachment vote, and the GOP scores a win in Texas

  • Jan. 6 panel divided over whether to issue criminal referral of Trump

  • Markets and households lose faith that Fed can handle inflation
  • SBA approved loans with signs of fraud early in pandemic, House report says
  • Coinbase’s layoffs suggest crypto’s problems are spreading
  • Warehouses in China and U.S. show global economy struggling to adjust
  • Why an energy crisis and $5 gas aren’t spurring a green revolution
  • The biggest news from the Xbox Games extended showcase
  • This AI model tries to re-create the mind of Ruth Bader Ginsburg
  • Children are targets for ID theft. Here’s what parents need to know.
  • How to add color to concrete
  • Finding inspiration and relaxation in luxury condos of Old Town North
  • What to consider before replacing the windows on your home
  • Mortgage rates, home prices expected to stabilize, forecast says
  • Putin Prepares to Declare Himself a Conqueror
  • Decisive People Aren’t Better Decision-Makers
  • Macron Tech Champion Atos Faces Brutal New M&A World
  • The World Can Stave Off Putin’s Food Fight
  • What Are Carbon Offsets and How Many Really Work?
  • The Boom in Private Markets Has Transformed Finance. Here’s How
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • The War in Ukraine Is Speeding Europe’s Pivot to Renewables
  • The good news about gun control isn’t the bipartisan deal
  • I thought the Jan. 6 committee wouldn’t matter. I was wrong.
  • The U.S. must oppose India’s rising Islamophobia
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • A wave of violence as the court prepares to rule
  • In Ukraine, is the balance tipping in Moscow’s favor? Not yet.
  • It’s almost as if Democrats are trying to make sure Trump wins in 2024
  • Is AI sentient? Wrong question.
  • Greed is dead. Long live greed!
  • Why prosecutorial reform will outlive Chesa Boudin’s recall
  • Michael R. Jackson’s ‘big, Black and queer’ musical, ‘A Strange Loop’

  • How aggressively should liberals attack the Supreme Court?
  • Americans have gone back to work. Now we can move toward steady growth.
  • Out of bounds
  • Has Justin Trudeau been ‘Americanized’ — or is he just left-wing?
  • I saw Mugabe wreck a democracy. The Jan. 6 hearings matter — a lot.
  • Fairfax County is a leader in promoting red-flag laws. Others should follow.
  • Is Virginia’s lieutenant governor paying attention to her friends?
  • Reparations should include funding for high-quality early education
  • A rise in tensions as the Supreme Court nears the end of its term
  • Mobile voting is not the answer to low turnout and disenfranchisement
  • Mental health care is adversely affected by focus on the bottom line
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Bairstow smashes century to set up England’s series win over New Zealand
  • Stephen Colbert: ‘How many of Trump’s terrible ideas came from an inebriated Giuliani?’
  • Vivian Maier: Anthology review – the eccentric nanny with an eye for a picture

  • What are the best Italian cookbooks? | Kitchen aide

  • Serena Williams granted wildcard into Wimbledon singles draw

  • Nigel Slater’s recipe for a tart of cherries and berries
  • ‘I could not be part of this crime’: the Russians fighting for Ukraine
  • ‘This is not hopeless’: the progressive prosecutors who vow not to enforce abortion bans
  • Africa must forgo gas exploration to avert climate disaster, warn experts
  • The 1977 White House climate memo that should have changed the world
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Indonesia’s Jokowi reshuffles Cabinet for the third time in his current term
  • How it came to this: The Fed and White House's slow inflation awakening
  • Australia suspends electricity market to stave off blackouts
  • US panel weighs authorising COVID-19 vaccines for youngest children
  • Top S Korean economic, finance officials to meet Thursday
  • Switzerland closes airspace after computer glitch
  • India approves proposal for private firms to operate 5G networks
  • BOJ offers to buy unlimited sum of JGBs with 7 years left until maturity
  • Tickets sold out for some peak-hour JB-Woodlands trains ahead of service resumption
  • Australia wants 'broad, deep' security ties with Japan, minister says
  • Chinese local govt financing units seek unorthodox route to raise funds
  • Credit Suisse shuffles Asia Pac banking team -memo
  • Amber Heard still in Aquaman 2 despite viral report claiming otherwise, reveals spokesperson
  • Indonesia trade surplus misses forecast on palm oil export ban
  • Europe's climate policies risk being weakened, 10 countries warn
  • China's middle class looks to flee as COVID-19 policies bite
  • Sentosa, RWS to install solar panels at 18 locations as part of carbon neutrality efforts
  • CNA remains most trusted news brand in Singapore: Reuters Institute report
  • England captain Stokes 'blown away' by test win at Trent Bridge
  • HungryGoWhere resumes operations after Grab buys, relaunches F&B site
  • South Korea truckers return to work after strike ends; shares in companies rise
  • US rebuffs China by calling Taiwan Strait an international waterway
  • Commentary: Under shadow of Ukraine war, Shangri-La Dialogue shifts from peace to power
  • 'It’s a downward spiral': Labour crunch hampers recovery of Malaysia’s hospitality sector despite border reopening
  • China attack on Taiwan would hit global trade more than Ukraine war, says Taiwan
  • South Korea truckers end strike; government agrees to extend pay guarantee
  • Children killed, tortured in Myanmar junta's crackdown, UN expert says
  • Shanghai Disney Resort to reopen Disneytown, hotel on Jun 16
  • India overhauls military recruitment, seeking younger troops
  • Taiwan World Cup fans likely to be listed as Chinese
  • Indonesia's new capital project will proceed, no matter 2024 election outcome: Senior Nusantara authority official
  • Shopee lays off staff in food delivery, online payment teams in Southeast Asia
  • Global economic recovery from COVID-19 overshadowed by food, energy supply disruptions: Indonesian finance minister
  • Polluted air cuts global life expectancy by two years
  • Hong Kong's famous Jumbo Floating Restaurant towed away after half a century
  • Sri Lanka gives public workers extra day off to grow food
  • Yen at risk of declining vs dollar into Q4 or later, economists say: Reuters poll
  • Glass Lewis opposes lawyer election to SoftBank board for second year
  • AirAsia X to add long-haul routes, including London, as demand rebounds
  • China's economic recovery to improve further in June - stats bureau
  • China May property sales fall at slower pace as policies buoy demand
  • Australian Federal Court approves Blackstone's $6.3 billion Crown Resorts deal
  • Vietnam central bank says bank lending as of June 9 up 8.15% from end-2021
  • China's CNOOC completes first offshore carbon capture site
  • Shanghai lockdown sees quarter of US firms cut investment plans: Poll
  • China's factories perk up, but frail consumption points to weak economic recovery
  • Japan government hopes BOJ takes 'necessary' action on yen, inflation
  • Shares sombre, dollar on a high as Fed seen going big
  • Vivian Balakrishnan in New Delhi for special meeting of India and ASEAN foreign ministers
  • Dengue outbreak: Wolbachia mosquito project to be expanded to 1,400 more HDB blocks following 'promising' results
  • She's hooked: The 19-year-old Singaporean crochet artist designing clothes and even S$333 bags
  • She didn't know she was autistic until she was 42. It changed her life
  • Sports Hub 'an isolated case'; other public-private partnerships in Singapore have worked well: Analysts
  • Singapore wrap up Asian Cup qualifying campaign with 6-2 thumping of Myanmar
  • Singapore may have access to new dengue vaccine in about a year, says veteran infectious diseases expert
  • Rare 'strawberry supermoon' seen over Singapore
  • Serial voyeur jailed for peeping at teen in Tanglin Shopping Centre toilet
  • Police warn of new phishing scam involving malware installed on victims' phones
  • Former NUS researcher admits making false claims for S$40,000 over 8 years
  • Former Singtel employee jailed for cheating friends into signing up for mobile plans
  • Disney film Lightyear, with ‘overt homosexual depictions’, to be released with NC16 rating: IMDA
  • Rossi relishes Hungary's 'miracle' win over England
  • Ireland to bring five uncapped players on New Zealand tour
  • New Zealand's Jamieson ruled out of third England test with back injury
  • Triple Olympic champion Wlodarczyk to miss rest of season
  • New Zealand coach Hay lashes out at ref, VAR after World Cup exit
  • Ferrari find 'short-term fix' for hydraulics problem
  • Five-star Germany crush Italy to snap winless run
  • Batshuayi on target as Belgium beat Poland
  • Collins wonder goal in vain as Ukraine hold Ireland
  • Ruthless Hungary stun dismal England with 4-0 away win
  • Depay nets late winner as Netherlands beat Wales 3-2
  • Former Hamburg defender Benjamin named new Namibia coach
  • Costa Rica book place at World Cup finals after playoff win
  • Bordeaux relegated to third tier due to financial difficulties

  • NATO needs greater readiness, more weapons: Military alliance chief
  • Russia bans 29 British journalists, along with defence figures
  • Ethiopia forms body to negotiate with rebellious Tigray forces

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 06:44AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • SCMP picks Catherine So as chief executive, tapping Hong Kong media and tech veteran to run one of Asia’s oldest English newspapers
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong bar and club patrons cancel bookings over rapid-test proof rule, as industry leader slams ‘unclear guidelines’
  • Hong Kong legislators question transport bureau’s proposal for congestion levy to ease traffic jams at cross-harbour tunnels
  • Hong Kong slips one place to fourth most expensive city for luxury spending as hotel rates take a huge hit from pandemic, Julius Baer report finds
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s first chief justice Andrew Li on whether all judges in city should be Chinese nationals ahead of 2047
  • Climate Change: China’s firms lag behind Asia, North America, Europe in net-zero targets with only 4 per cent adherence, study finds
  • Historic Hong Kong mansion handed over to pro-Beijing think tank to transform into healthy living centre
  • February 8, 2016: Night of mob violence in Mong Kok leaves Hong Kong on edge
  • Beijing reveals blueprint for Nansha, with incentives that could draw young Hongkongers into calling the Guangzhou district home
  • Zuckerberg’s Meta to push metaverse initiatives to Hong Kong cafes and schools as interest in virtual world hots up
  • November 1, 2014: Expat banker Rurik Jutting arrested over murder of 2 women
  • HKMA sells US$1.74 billion to defend Hong Kong currency peg from capital outflow ahead of expected US interest rate rise
  • October 1, 2012: Deadly ferry crash off Hong Kong’s Lamma Island kills 39
  • Cathay Pacific expecting better showing in first half of 2022 despite staying in the red, after strong cargo performance and cost-cutting moves
  • Hong Kong health officials change how they present daily number of Covid-19 infections
  • August 23, 2010: 7 Hong Kong tourists and guide killed in Manila bus hostage crisis
  • Hong Kong customs seizes phones, pharmaceuticals in haul worth HK$7.5 million hidden in excavators
  • July 1, 2003: 500,000 take to Hong Kong’s streets in protest against proposed national security legislation
  • China’s internet watchdog tightens mobile app rules for national security, requires promotion of ‘core socialist values’
  • As China’s economic slowdown moderates, analysts call for greater policy easing: ‘it will be a long haul back’
  • China refrains from policy rate cut, defying market expectations once again
  • How China’s belt and road diplomacy could end the Ukraine conflict for good
  • Veteran Chinese human rights lawyers expected to face trial soon
  • Chubb CEO Evan Greenberg urges US businesses to stay engaged in China market
  • Backlash against Russians living abroad is all too familiar for anxious Chinese expats
  • For China’s elite graduates, career dreams take a back seat to stability in coronavirus-hit job market
  • US drug supply chain alternative to China might run through Canada, Latin America
  • Chinese students’ dream device defeats Japan’s most powerful supercomputer in world contest
  • Dozens of countries call out China at UN over Xinjiang abuse allegations
  • Where did water on the moon come from? Chinese scientists find clues in lunar samples
  • Apple CEO Tim Cook expects continued growth for ‘cutting-edge’ Chinese software developers amid rising geopolitical tensions
  • China’s consumption problem rooted in fears and uncertainties, while vouchers have limited appeal
  • Chinese concerns rise over US policy shift on Taiwan
  • China still backing Poland gateway plan despite impact of Ukraine war
  • Career diplomat Le Yucheng moves to Chinese state broadcasting body as deputy chief
  • Coronavirus: Taiwan’s economic outlook dims as Omicron outbreak casts a shadow over growth
  • Chinese AI giant Megvii’s chief scientist Sun Jian dies at age 45 of sudden illness
  • China says it has released Bloomberg staffer Haze Fan on bail
  • Omicron sub-variants latest challenge for China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China calls for overseas arrivals to be monitored for signs of monkeypox
  • Chinese smartphone brand Meizu sold to carmaker Geely as smart vehicles become latest frontier for Big Tech
  • Fears of data abuse as Chinese health code turns red for financial scandal protesters
  • OnlyFans couple sought by Thai police over ‘unacceptable’ sex videos that were leaked online
  • Indonesia’s Joko Widodo to meet Vladimir Putin in Moscow on June 30: Russian report
  • Asia’s energy crunch turns from bad to worse after fire at US plant
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra’s foreign minister Penny Wong to travel to Solomon Islands amid China security pact concerns
  • Deaf Indian boy trapped in well with snakes and scorpions is rescued after four days
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand to eliminate pre-departure Covid-19 tests for incoming passengers
  • India all but sinks WTO sustainable fishing deal; China among contributors to global overfishing
  • Can Sri Lanka tourism recover from ‘triple whammy’ of terrorism, Covid-19 and economic crisis?
  • BTS announce temporary break, with plans for solo releases before reuniting as group – though label denies it’s a ‘hiatus’
  • Are India’s billion-dollar trade ties with Gulf states in ‘peril’ amid Prophet Mohammed row?
  • Thai cat with Covid suspected to have infected human
  • Malaysia eases chicken export ban but not for Singapore’s favourite ‘broiler’
  • Take holiday to grow your own food, civil servants told by Sri Lankan government
  • AirAsia turns to India to fill gap left by absent China tourists due to Covid-19 travel curbs
  • Iko Uwais, Indonesian martial arts star who rose to fame in Hollywood and Netflix’s Wu Assassins accused of assaulting interior designer
  • India’s women, Muslims targeted by nationalist YouTube influencers, report finds
  • Ukraine war: Russia offers evacuations from Sievierodonetsk chemical plant, where 500 civilians are taking shelter
  • Who’s coming to Putin’s ‘Russian Davos’ in St Petersburg?
  • Russia turns to old tanks as it burns through weapons in Ukraine, European officials say
  • In Ukraine-Russia war, echoes of WWI with artillery duels, trenches and shell-blasted landscapes
  • Netflix plans Squid Game reality show with US$4.5 million prize, no fatal consequences
  • Canada and Denmark settle decades-old ‘whiskey war’ over Arctic island
  • UK vows to pursue asylum seeker policy after first deportation flight to Rwanda cancelled
  • Iran preparing for rocket launch, satellite images suggest amid nuclear tensions
  • Jailed Putin critic Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Happy the elephant is not a person, top New York court rules – she is a ‘magnificent creature’
  • EU turns to Israel for gas after ‘blackmail’ by Russia over Ukraine war support
  • Pope says Russia’s ‘ferocious’ invasion perhaps provoked and we don’t see the ‘whole drama’
  • Ukraine war: ‘Surrender or die’ choice in Sievierodonetsk after Russian forces destroy bridges to city
  • Gruesome scenes and anguish in Ukraine frontline city Lysychansk: ‘They bomb and bomb’
  • Why rising interest rates are bad news for Hong Kong’s housing market
  • Google debate over sentient AI overshadows more pressing issues like prejudice and exploitation, researchers say
  • Emigrating mainland Chinese and Hongkongers could take combined wealth of US$65 billion with them, consultancy says
  • China’s US$20 trillion asset management industry to embrace more diverse products amid revamp, think tank says
  • Alibaba leads Hong Kong stocks to best gain in a week as China data shows traction in recovery as markets brace for Fed rate hike
  • US likely to single out Tesla for crashes involving Autopilot after collecting data on automated driving
  • China releases new national climate change strategy to build resilience against global warming by 2035
  • China to test market appetite for debt with biggest offshore bond sale since 2016
  • In Hong Kong’s property market, HK$10 million no longer buys what it did in 1997
  • Developers’ aggressive loan schemes could put financially weak buyers at risk if Hong Kong home prices fall, analysts say
  • Hong Kong malls, retailers tap into AI analytics to get smarter after losing US$16 billion of sales in post-2018 industry slump
  • China’s 618 online shopping festival more muted than previous years as platforms shift focus from sales to service quality
  • As the industrial metaverse grows, the platform must remain open and available
  • Yuan currency most exposed to climate change – could lose half its value in 50 years, Barclays says
  • US senator questions whether HSBC was pressured to suspend banker over controversial climate-change comments
  • GoGoX defies IPO drought as it pushes through downsized Hong Kong offering targeting US$85.5 million, down from US$500 million
  • Shanghai merchants, hard-hit by Covid-19 lockdown, delay reopening of Qipu Road clothing market, demand rent relief
  • SoftBank plans additional London listing for Arm initial public offering
  • ByteDance’s Hong Kong entity registers multiple mainland subsidiaries in move seen as prelude to possible listing in the city
  • From minimalism to maximalist design, Singapore apartment full of vintage furniture, collectibles and art is a delight for the senses
  • The best cart noodles in Hong Kong according to a chef, where he goes for spare ribs rice, and memorable meals with his boss, ‘Demon Chef’ Alvin Leung
  • Airport chaos as surge in flight bookings catches travel trade on the hop, with long queues and cancellations
  • What is amla? The Indian gooseberry hailed as a superfood for its apparent anticancer, anti-diabetes and anti-ageing properties
  • The Black Phone movie review: Ethan Hawke plays a sadistic child murderer in Doctor Strange director’s effective horror thriller
  • Are bleached eyebrows right for you? Expert tips on a viral ’90s beauty trend made popular by Kendall Jenner, Maisie Williams and Lizzo
  • Netflix K-drama review: Our Blues – Lee Byung-hun and Kim Hye-ja provide heart-rending ending for ensemble drama
  • Blood donation is easy and painless, says record donor in Hong Kong, who’s 70 and has given blood 190 times. How and why you should too
  • Japanese artist who was an ‘Instagram star’ years before the internet was born shows works in Hong Kong
  • The 15 best new Hollywood movies in cinemas this summer, from Elvis to Minions: The Rise of Gru and Thor: Love and Thunder
  • 6 K-pop groups who stayed together after leaving their agencies: Got7, 2PM, Girls’ Generation, Mamamoo, Apink and Highlight all made comebacks with their OG members
  • Meet Kim Se-jeong, Today’s Webtoon star: the K-drama actress debuted as a K-pop idol in IOI before acting in K-dramas like Business Proposal on Netflix – but why is she called ‘Korea’s Emma Stone’?
  • Zhou Rui’s provocative fashion: she’s dressed Blackpink’s Lisa, Madison Beer and other stars within just three years of launching her brand thanks to her style that ‘isn’t limited by gender or size’
  • Meet Riley Keough, Elvis Presley’s granddaughter – she modelled for Dior before acting and directing, and ‘loved’ stepdad Michael Jackson, but why didn’t she act in the Elvis biopic?
  • Who is Rachel Zegler, the new lead in Hunger Games’ prequel? She got her break in Steven Spielberg’s West Side Story, walks red carpets in Dior, and covers Lady Gaga on YouTube
  • Savoury desserts? Fine dining chefs are giving sweet treats a new take, from Michelin-starred Noma in Denmark’s dish with oyster stock, to Sawaan in Thailand’s crab claw-inspired concept
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ECB calls unscheduled meeting to discuss market turmoil
  • ECB has ‘no limits’ in defence of euro from debt turmoil, says executive

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden Weighs Tariff Rollback to Ease Inflation, Even a Little Bit
